category_title: Students with limited English proficiency who communicate knowledge through visual arts
minimal_context: The student is working on constructing a summative project using artwork in art class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please continue working on constructing your summative project.

STUDENT: (Drawing) I am making my artwork. Collecting information and finding the English vocabulary was hard.

TUTOR: That part of your drawing is wrong.

STUDENT: Wrong? I wanted to communicate my knowledge through drawing.

TUTOR: Change it to look like this picture in the book.

STUDENT: (Erases drawing) Okay. I will just regurgitate the information from the book and take your specific directive.

options.A: Allow the student to work on the artwork at their own pace and take breaks whenever desired.
options.B: Use open-ended, thought-provoking questions to guide the student in developing their summative project artwork.
options.C: Divide the summative project into small chunks and provide the student with one chunk at a time.
options.D: Model the basics of drawing a specific shape for the project and have the student practice filling it in.
